question: Which ecoregion has the most conserved acreage?

turns.name: get_schema
turns.result_preview:

turns.name: get_schema
turns.result_preview:
**California 30x30 Ecoregions (CNRA / ACE Bailey-derived, 2024)**
Collection ID: `ca30x30-ecoregion`
collection_url: `https://s3-west.nrp-nautilus.io/public-ca30x30/ecoregion/stac-collection.json`
California ecoregions used by the state's 30x30 conservation initiative, derived from the USDA Forest Service Bailey/ECOMAP ecological sections and distributed by the California Department of Fish and Wildlife (CDFW) Areas of Conservation Emphasis (ACE) program via the California Natural Resources Agen

turns.name: query
turns.arguments:
{"sql_query":"SELECT
  CA_Ecoregi AS ecoregion,
  SUM(Acres) AS conserved_acres,
  SUM(Acres) / 43560.0 AS conserved_acres_formatted
FROM read_parquet('s3://public-ca30x30/conserved-areas-terrestrial-2025.parquet')
GROUP BY CA_Ecoregi
ORDER BY conserved_acres DESC"}

turns.name: query
turns.result_preview:

response:
The **Mojave Desert** ecoregion has the most conserved acreage, with approximately **7.37 million acres** (GAP 1+2) counted toward the 30x30 goal.

Here's the full ranking:

| Ecoregion | Conserved Acres (GAP 1+2) |
|---|---|
| **Mojave Desert** | **7,373,400** |
| Sierra Nevada | 4,638,090 |
| Southern California Mountains and Valleys | 2,479,490 |
| Southeastern Great Basin | 1,988,870 |
| Sonoran Desert | 1,626,090 |
| Klamath Mountains | 1,186,000 |
| Central Valley Coast Ranges | 989,895 |
| Central California Coast | 822,701 |
| Northern California Coast | 804,429 |
| Northern California Coast Ranges | 677,644 |
| Southern Cascades | 566,605 |
| Sierra Nevada Foothills | 523,978 |
| Mono | 514,349 |
| Colorado Desert | 448,997 |
| Southern California Coast | 429,015 |
| Great Valley (South) | 416,088 |
| Great Valley (North) | 387,921 |
| Modoc Plateau | 270,137 |
| Northwestern Basin and Range | 196,386 |
| Northern California Interior Coast Ranges | 131,371 |

The Mojave Desert alone accounts for roughly 28% of all conserved acreage statewide. The top three ecoregions (Mojave Desert, Sierra Nevada, and Southern California Mountains and Valleys) together hold about 60% of the state's conserved lands.
